capital_adjusted: R1 Picks 1-10; Two-way premium; capital reflects top-side CB/WR position; Tier A confidence
strengths: Generationally unique two-way player with elite CB and WR production in the same season — a feat not accomplished at Power 5 level in decades. 9.5 athleticism profile combines top-end speed (sub-4.4 projection), elite jump ball skills, and exceptional change-of-direction. Two-Way Premium reflects genuine draft capital elevation: whichever side he plays, he profiles as a top-10 pick at that position.
red_flags: NFL teams must commit to one position; transition risk is real regardless of skill set. Two-way college workload means fewer reps in pure CB technique fundamentals (press technique, trail technique, jam footwork) compared to a single-position prospect. COMPRESSION gap reflects his elite balance across all archetypes rather than a concern — no dominant archetype because all are elite.

capital_adjusted: R1 Day 2 (R2 top) / R2; Tier C — ±1 round range, monitor; system dependency discount applied
strengths: Elite touch and accuracy from the pocket, NFL-ready release mechanics, and exceptional ability to navigate pressure and extend plays within his frame. Strong statistical output at Colorado against Power 4 competition demonstrates adaptability beyond his JSU baseline. Process as a passer, specifically his pre-snap identification and quick release reads, is genuinely high-floor.
red_flags: System dependency is the primary concern — Colorado's offense was architecturally designed around Sanders' skill set under Deion Sanders' bespoke scheme. Tier C confidence reflects legitimate unknowns: independent coaching relationship (Deion factor reduces confidence in c2/c3 baseline accuracy), limited scheme-diverse reps, and questions about translating production to an NFL system he did not design. RB athleticism profile limits scramble/extend threat value significantly. Capital carries ±1 round Tier C range.

capital_adjusted: R1 Picks 11-32 / R1 Top depending on team need; Tier A confidence
strengths: Young and physically imposing OT with elite athleticism for the position — long arms, exceptional anchor strength, and outstanding short-area burst that stresses interior defenders. Missouri background provided meaningful SEC-adjacent competition, and Membou dominated in both run and pass protection. Developmental trajectory is among the best in this class: already dominant at a young age with clear ceiling remaining.
red_flags: COMPRESSION gap indicates he profiles well in multiple archetypes (athletic projection and complete prospect) without a dominant singular identity — a positive signal for this class. Still developing elite hand technique and combat consistency in pass rush, which will be stress-tested in the NFL. Limited power conference playoff depth at Missouri reduces SOS confidence.

capital_adjusted: Late R1 / R2 top; Tier A confidence
strengths: Elite run blocker with outstanding leverage, power, and contact-seeking mentality — every NFL run-first coordinator's ideal interior blocker. Four-year starter at Georgia with consistent dominance in SEC competition against NFL-caliber defensive linemen. Excellent competitive toughness and effort tail that elevates every snap.
red_flags: Athletic profile for an OG is solid but not exceptional; limited to run-heavy scheme alignments in full exploitation. Pass protection against elite speed rushers off the edge is his biggest projection concern — tendency to overextend hand placement under pressure. SOLID archetype gap is clean but not elite, reflecting a clear fit without the upside ceiling of the COMPRESSION tier.

capital_adjusted: R2 / R3 top; Tier A confidence
strengths: Well-rounded TE prospect with above-average athleticism and reliable hands in contested catch situations. Texas system provided meaningful opportunities against Big 12 pass rushers and coverage. Effective in both seam routes and in-line blocking, making him a viable two-way TE at the NFL level. TWEENER gap reflects balance between receiving-first and complete archetypes.
red_flags: Not an elite separator — relies on size and contested-catch ability rather than route precision or YAC creation. Blocking technique needs refinement at the point of attack against NFL-caliber defensive ends. His overall profile projects as a quality starter but not a top-5 TE in this class. Draft capital reflects good but not elite upside.

capital_adjusted: Day 3 / R4-R5 range; Tier B — ±0.5 round range; RB positional discount applied
strengths: Good short-area quickness and burst behind pads, effective in zone-read and inside zone concepts. Georgia production reflects good efficiency when healthy and playing behind an elite offensive line. Legitimate pass-catching ability from the backfield is a positive NFL-era trait.
red_flags: NO_FIT archetype gap reflects a profile that doesn't dominate any specific RB archetype — not elite enough to anchor as a feature back, not specialized enough to be a true satellite/receiving back, not physical enough as a between-the-tackles specialist. PVC of 0.70 (lowest positional premium) reflects market reality. Tier B confidence due to transfer context and inconsistent production volume. DEVELOPMENTAL tier is appropriate given the current trait-to-production ceiling alignment; projection requires scheme-specific development.

capital_adjusted: Late R1 / R2 top; SOS Gate note — production confidence Tier B; ±0.5 round range
strengths: Elite athletic profile for the safety position — outstanding speed, range, and zone coverage ability that projects to premium box safety or single-high roles. South Carolina production showed consistent effort and playmaking against SEC competition. Physical profile and processing speed make him a legitimate early-round safety in the post-Ed Reed era of the position.
red_flags: SOS Gate applied: South Carolina schedule strength in 2025 did not include consistent elite competition, creating uncertainty in the production-quality floor. v_production discounted from raw assessment by -1.5 to reflect this. Tier B confidence due to SOS limitations. COMPRESSION gap reflects a complete safety profile without a singular dominant trait — athleticism leads but processing and versatility are nearly equal; this is a positive signal, not a concern.

capital_adjusted: Late R1 / R2; Tier A confidence; production-led profile
strengths: Elite production from the interior at Ohio State against Big Ten competition — consistently penetrated against NFL offensive linemen and generated wins both against the run and in pass rush. Exceptional competitive toughness; does not take snaps off, high motor in pursuit. Athleticism profile for an IDL is above average with a quick first step and leverage advantage.
red_flags: SOLID archetype gap (not COMPRESSION) reflects a player who is clearly a Production Machine without elite versatility or developmental upside ceiling. IDL PVC of 0.90 reflects positional market reality. Route to elite production in the NFL requires continued counter-move development — currently relies heavily on power and leverage rather than diverse pass rush plan.

capital_adjusted: R2 / R3 top; Tier A confidence; C positional discount reflected
strengths: Elite center intelligence — outstanding pre-snap communication, protection call identification, and line calls make him a natural for NFL green-dot center duties. Pittsburgh background developed a complete center skill set across both run and pass protection. Processing speed at the center position is among the best in the 2026 class; veteran coaches consistently praise his film study.
red_flags: Athletic profile is serviceable but below elite for the position — lacks the burst to pull effectively in stretch schemes or reach block against explosive 3-techs. C PVC of 0.80 reflects positional market reality; centers rarely command premium capital regardless of quality. Production at Pitt (ACC competition) did not consistently stress-test against Big Ten or SEC interior talent.

capital_adjusted: R2 / R3; Tier A confidence; C positional discount; production-led profile
strengths: Dominant run blocker at Georgia against elite SEC competition — powered the Bulldogs' run game with consistent positional dominance at the point of attack. Outstanding physical profile for the center position with excellent anchor strength and hand power. SEC championship experience provides elite competition floor confidence.
red_flags: Processing and protection call intelligence is good but below elite — projects more as a run-first center than a pure green-dot communicator, which limits upside in pass-heavy schemes. C PVC of 0.80 reflects market reality. Development in pass protection technique (hand replacement, set mechanics under speed) is the key NFL translation variable.

capital_adjusted: R1 Picks 11-32
strengths: Elite athleticism with 9.84 RAS creates unmatched versatility in modern defensive schemes. Processing ability allows him to diagnose plays quickly and cover significant ground sideline-to-sideline. Positional flexibility enables deployment in multiple roles including coverage, blitz packages, and traditional linebacker duties.
red_flags: Production inconsistency relative to elite athletic profile suggests technique refinement needed. Some missed tackles in space when attacking at bad angles. Needs to develop more consistent gap discipline in run fits to maximize impact at next level.

capital_adjusted: R2 - Tier B ±0.5 round range
strengths: Physical run defender with excellent gap discipline and sideline-to-sideline pursuit ability. Shows consistent tackling form and rarely misses in the run game. Demonstrates good instincts for finding the football and flows well to the ball carrier.
red_flags: Limited coverage skills against pass-catching backs and tight ends in space. Processing speed can be slow on complex pass concepts. May struggle in sub packages where coverage responsibilities increase significantly.

capital_adjusted: R1 Top (early) - Tier B ±0.5 round range
strengths: Elite athletic profile with 9.87 RAS showcases rare speed-agility combination for ILB position. Demonstrates positional versatility with ability to cover backs and tight ends in man coverage while also showing effective blitz packages. High motor player with consistent effort level and developmental trajectory trending upward.
red_flags: Production numbers don't fully match the elite athletic testing, suggesting some refinement needed in processing and instincts. Limited high-level competition exposure in Pac-12 creates some projection risk. Gap-fitting consistency and block-shedding technique still developing despite physical tools.

capital_adjusted: R1 Picks 1-10
strengths: Elite athletic testing with 9.99 RAS combines with advanced processing ability and pre-snap recognition. Commands the defense with green dot communication skills and shows exceptional range in coverage. Physical enough to handle run fits while maintaining coverage responsibility across multiple levels.
red_flags: Limited multi-year starting sample at Ohio State creates some projection risk despite elite measurables. Question marks around consistency against elite competition in biggest games. Needs to prove he can handle the mental load of NFL-level protection calls and adjustments.

capital_adjusted: R3 Tier B — ±0.5 round range
strengths: Physical point-of-attack defender who excels in gap fitting and run support. Strong competitive toughness and effort level with consistent production against Big 12 competition. Shows good instincts in identifying run concepts and attacking downhill.
red_flags: Below-average athleticism significantly limits coverage range and pass defense versatility. Processing speed can be inconsistent on complex passing concepts. Limited scheme versatility may restrict NFL deployment to run-heavy packages.

capital_adjusted: R1 Day 2 (R2) - Tier B — ±0.5 round range
strengths: Physical run defender with elite gap-fitting instincts and consistent tackling form. Shows strong competitive drive and motor that translates to steady production against SEC competition. Reliable in short-yardage situations with good pursuit angles.
red_flags: Limited range in coverage situations and struggles to match athletic tight ends and running backs in space. Processing speed against play-action and misdirection needs improvement. Lacks the elite athleticism to develop into a three-down linebacker at the NFL level.

capital_adjusted: R2 - Tier B ±0.5 round range
strengths: Physical run defender with excellent gap discipline and sideline-to-sideline pursuit ability. Shows strong competitive toughness and motor consistency throughout games. Reliable tackler with solid production metrics against Big 12 competition.
red_flags: Processing speed can be inconsistent on complex play-action concepts and RPO reads. Limited experience in diverse coverage packages beyond basic zone responsibilities. Tier B confidence due to some gaps in high-level competition exposure.

capital_adjusted: R1 mid; APEX composite; OT PVC applied; Tier A confidence
strengths: Premium OT profile with elite size and length. Miami (FL) competition against elite pass rushers gives high-quality film sample. Power and run-block leverage are prototypical for the position. Consistent performer across full season without major technical breakdowns.
red_flags: OT PVC (0.90) suppression. Pass set footwork is functional but has refinement upside — speed edge rushers can threaten with speed-to-power on current technique. Duplicate DB entry should be resolved before final capital assignment.

capital_adjusted: R1 Picks 11-32 + Walk-On premium
strengths: Elite pre-snap processor who commands the defense with advanced recognition skills and communication ability. Former walk-on with exceptional work ethic and motor, displaying consistent gap-fitting technique and reliable tackling mechanics. Shows outstanding football IQ with ability to diagnose plays quickly and adjust coverage responsibilities mid-snap.
red_flags: Limited elite-level athleticism may struggle against fastest NFL skill position players in space. Occasional missed tackles when pursuing sideline-to-sideline, and size limitations could be exploited by power running schemes. Needs to prove durability over full NFL season workload.

capital_adjusted: R1 Picks 11-32 - Two-way premium; capital reflects top-side position
strengths: Exceptional size-speed combination at 6'1" 185 with sub-4.4 speed creating elite physical tools for coverage. Demonstrates remarkable competitive toughness playing both ways at elite level with transfer portal adversity response. Elite ball skills and catch-point dominance against top-tier Pac-12 receivers.
red_flags: Processing still developing with occasional late recognition in complex route concepts. Two-way workload creates injury risk and potential technique refinement delays. Transfer situation and coaching changes may impact scheme familiarity and consistency.

capital_adjusted: R1 Top (early)
strengths: Elite pre-snap processor with exceptional pocket presence and timing. Demonstrates high-level anticipation throws and command of complex offensive systems. Accurate across all levels of the field with consistent decision-making under pressure.
red_flags: Limited elite athleticism may restrict scrambling ability and off-platform throws. Questions about leadership approach and coachability given family dynamics and transfer circumstances. Needs to prove durability against NFL-level pass rush.

capital_adjusted: R2 early - Tier B ±0.5 round range
strengths: Elite physical tools with impressive length and athleticism that projects well to NFL level. Strong work ethic and coachability suggest significant upside with proper development. Clean injury history and character profile make him a safe investment.
red_flags: Processing and technique refinement needed against elite pass rushers. Limited experience against top-tier competition may inflate current projections. Scheme versatility questions due to primarily gap-scheme experience at Missouri.

capital_adjusted: R1 Top (early)
strengths: Elite technician with exceptional hand placement and footwork who dominated both pass protection and run blocking in the SEC. Shows advanced processing skills with consistent identification of stunts and twists, plus the versatility to excel in multiple blocking schemes. Brings high-level competitive toughness and reliability as a multi-year starter against elite competition.
red_flags: Athletic limitations may cap ceiling against elite NFL pass rushers with superior bend and speed. Injury history includes some missed time that bears monitoring for durability concerns. May need time to adjust to the increased speed and complexity of NFL defensive fronts despite strong college production.

capital_adjusted: R2 / R3 top; Tier A confidence
strengths: Well-rounded TE prospect with above-average athleticism and reliable hands in contested catch situations. Texas system provided meaningful opportunities against Big 12 pass rushers and coverage. Effective in both seam routes and in-line blocking, making him a viable two-way TE at the NFL level. TWEENER gap reflects balance between receiving-first and complete archetypes.
red_flags: Not an elite separator â€” relies on size and contested-catch ability rather than route precision or YAC creation. Draft capital reflects good but not elite upside.

capital_adjusted: R2 - Tier B ±0.5 round range, positional value ceiling
strengths: Elite between-the-tackles runner with exceptional vision and contact balance. Demonstrates reliable hands as a receiver and solid pass protection awareness. Shows excellent competitive toughness and consistently performs in high-pressure SEC games.
red_flags: Limited top-end breakaway speed and some durability concerns with moderate injury history. Transfer from Florida raises questions about scheme fit consistency. Position value at running back limits draft capital ceiling regardless of talent level.

capital_adjusted: R1 Top (early), Tier B — ±0.5 round range
strengths: Physical enforcer with exceptional run support instincts and contact courage. Elite tackler who brings consistent physicality in the box and shows strong processing against run concepts. Natural leader with high motor who elevates his effort in big games.
red_flags: Limited deep coverage range and struggles with vertical routes from slot receivers. Processing can be late against play-action concepts and lacks elite ball skills for centerfield role. SEC competition level provides some confidence but limited exposure to elite passing offenses.

capital_adjusted: R1 Day 2 (R2), Tier B — ±0.5 round range
strengths: Elite get-off and bend around the edge with natural pass rush instincts and motor that never stops. Generates consistent pressure with speed-to-power conversion and shows excellent pursuit angles in run defense. Production speaks for itself with multiple double-digit sack seasons.
red_flags: Limited hand technique development and counter package consistency against elite tackles. Run defense can be inconsistent when asked to set firm edge against power schemes. Needs to add functional strength and leverage principles to maximize NFL ceiling.

capital_adjusted: R1 Day 2 (R2) - Tier B ±0.5 round range
strengths: Elite physical tools with outstanding length and leverage at 6'3" 325 lbs. Demonstrates consistent motor and effort through contact with strong hands to shed blocks. Shows versatility to impact both run defense and pass rush from multiple alignments along the defensive front.
red_flags: Production numbers don't fully match physical ability, suggesting technique refinement needed. Limited experience as primary focal point of opposing game plans. Needs to develop more consistent pass rush moves beyond initial power and length.

capital_adjusted: R1 Day 2 (R2) - Tier B ±0.5 round range
strengths: Strong athletic foundation for the center position with good mobility and movement skills in zone concepts. Demonstrates solid competitive toughness and reliability, playing through contact consistently. Shows developing technique with room for significant growth under NFL coaching.
red_flags: Limited experience against elite interior defensive linemen in ACC play. Processing and pre-snap operation still developing for NFL center demands. Production metrics suggest inconsistency in sustained blocking effectiveness against top competition.

capital_adjusted: R1 Day 2 (R2) Tier B — ±0.5 round range
strengths: Wilson brings SEC-tested physicality and run-blocking power to the center position, demonstrating strong anchor ability against elite interior pass rushers. His competitive toughness and reliability as a starter in Georgia's championship-contending program shows he can handle pressure situations. Processing skills are solid with good pre-snap recognition and line calls in a complex offensive system.
red_flags: Athletic limitations may restrict his ceiling in zone-blocking schemes that require significant movement and pull assignments. Limited college production data makes projection challenging, and development trajectory appears modest without significant year-over-year improvement. May struggle against smaller, quicker interior rushers who can exploit his lateral movement limitations.

capital_adjusted: R1 Picks 11-32
strengths: Elite pre-snap processor with exceptional command of the offense and ability to diagnose coverage quickly. Demonstrates consistent accuracy across all levels of the field with strong arm talent to make throws off-platform. Shows remarkable composure in high-pressure situations and has led Indiana to unprecedented success with clutch performances in big games.
red_flags: Limited elite-level athleticism may restrict scrambling ability and extend-the-play capabilities compared to modern dual-threat quarterbacks. Questions remain about translating success from Indiana's system to NFL-level complexity against faster, more athletic defensive backs. Relatively limited sample size of high-pressure playoff-caliber games at the highest competition level.

capital_adjusted: R1 Picks 1-10
strengths: Elite deep coverage instincts with exceptional ball skills and range as the last line of defense. Demonstrates advanced pre-snap processing and route recognition, consistently putting himself in position to make impact plays. Shows excellent versatility in deployment across multiple safety alignments with natural leadership presence.
red_flags: Limited elite competition exposure in first college season before transfer to Ohio State. Some questions about consistency in run support responsibilities when force defender. Measurables still unknown pending combine testing which could impact athleticism evaluation.

capital_adjusted: R1 Picks 11-32
strengths: Elite combination of linear athleticism and hand technique allows him to win both as pass rusher and run defender on same drives. Exceptional motor and competitive toughness translate to consistent production against top competition. High processing ability enables quick diagnosis of blocking schemes and appropriate counter selection.
red_flags: Limited injury history with minor soft tissue issues that cost some practice time. Technique refinement needed in some counter sequences, though foundation is strong. May need time to adjust to increased strength and sophistication of NFL offensive lines.

capital_adjusted: R1 Picks 1-10, Tier B — ±0.5 round range
strengths: Elite production against Big 12 competition with dominant pass rush arsenal including speed-to-power conversion and developed hand technique. Exceptional motor and competitive toughness, consistently disrupts both run and pass concepts on the same drives. High-level processing allows him to diagnose plays quickly and adjust rush lanes accordingly.
red_flags: Limited high-level competition exposure in Big 12 may inflate some production metrics. Needs to prove technique translates against elite offensive line talent at next level. Some concerns about consistency of rush plan execution in biggest games.

capital_adjusted: R2 Tier B — ±0.5 round range
strengths: Elite physical tools with impressive length and speed that allow him to recover from early positioning mistakes. Shows natural ball skills and ability to make contested catches when targeted. Physical presence in press coverage with developing technique that projects well with NFL coaching.
red_flags: Processing and route recognition still developing, sometimes late to breaks and relies on athleticism to recover. Limited exposure to top-tier competition in recent seasons affects projection confidence. Needs refinement in zone coverage principles and anticipation skills.

capital_adjusted: R1 Picks 11-32
strengths: Elite route precision and release technique create consistent separation at all three levels. Outstanding body control and hands in traffic with reliable contested catch ability. Advanced understanding of leverage and timing allows him to win against press and off coverage consistently.
red_flags: Limited deep speed may cap vertical separation ability against elite corners. Needs to develop more consistent YAC creation after catch. Competition level questions remain as primary production came against Big Ten secondaries rather than elite SEC/ACC cornerbacks.

capital_adjusted: R1 Picks 11-32
strengths: Elite combination of pass protection anchor ability and run blocking power, with exceptional technique refinement and scheme versatility. Demonstrates consistent dominance against top-tier ACC competition with remarkable competitive toughness and finishing ability through contact.
red_flags: Limited explosive athleticism compared to modern zone-scheme demands may restrict scheme fit at next level. Developmental trajectory appears to be plateauing after strong early career growth, suggesting current ceiling may be close to maximum potential.

capital_adjusted: R1 Top (early) - Tier B — ±0.5 round range
strengths: Elite route precision and timing with quarterbacks, creating consistent separation at all three levels through technique rather than pure speed. Strong hands and catch radius in contested situations, with reliable production against Power 4 competition. High football IQ shows in route adjustments and finding soft spots in zone coverage.
red_flags: Limited elite burst and separation speed may struggle against top-tier NFL cornerbacks in press coverage. Needs to prove he can create separation when route timing is disrupted. Questions about ceiling as a true #1 receiver due to athletic limitations, though technique and processing suggest solid floor as complementary piece.

capital_adjusted: R1 Picks 11-32
strengths: Elite anticipatory processor who consistently jumps routes before the break, demonstrating exceptional pre-snap recognition and route concept understanding. Physical press coverage technique with outstanding competitive toughness, rarely backing down from contested situations. Scheme versatility allows deployment in multiple coverage concepts with consistent execution across different defensive systems.
red_flags: Athletic measurables may not test elite at combine despite solid college production and technique. Development trajectory has plateaued somewhat after strong early career growth. Limited exposure to top-tier receiver talent in ACC play may inflate some coverage metrics.

capital_adjusted: R1 Top (early) - Tier B ±0.5 round range
strengths: Elite route precision and field awareness with excellent hands in traffic. Shows advanced understanding of leverage and timing in the seam game. Demonstrates reliable blocking fundamentals with willingness to engage in the run game.
red_flags: Limited elite competition exposure in Pac-12 schedule raises questions about projection against top-tier NFL defenses. Route tree depth and contested catch ability need verification at higher levels of competition.

capital_adjusted: R1 Top (early)
strengths: Elite first-step quickness and penetration ability with natural pass rush instincts from the interior. Shows excellent hand usage and leverage technique for his age, consistently disrupting pocket timing and creating negative plays. Exceptional motor and effort level with the athletic profile to win in multiple ways against interior offensive linemen.
red_flags: Needs to add functional strength to handle double teams more consistently at the next level. Can be susceptible to getting washed out of running lanes when anchoring becomes priority over penetration. Limited sample size of elite production against top-tier offensive line competition raises questions about ceiling projection.

capital_adjusted: R1 Picks 11-32, Tier B — ±0.5 round range
strengths: Elite route precision and separation creation at all three levels with advanced understanding of leverage and timing. Shows exceptional ability to find soft spots in zone coverage and create separation through route nuance rather than pure speed. Demonstrates reliable hands and concentration in contested situations with strong production against quality competition.
red_flags: Athletic testing will be critical as film shows adequate but not elite speed or explosion which could limit ceiling against NFL-caliber defensive backs. Transfer portal move raises minor questions about program fit and development trajectory. Limited exposure to elite cornerback competition in Pac-12 could inflate some metrics.

capital_adjusted: R1 Picks 16-25 - Man coverage floor: confirmed
strengths: Elite physical press coverage with exceptional length and contact timing at the line of scrimmage. Demonstrated ability to shut down top-tier receivers in man coverage with strong competitive toughness. Shows developing anticipatory instincts that suggest a realistic pathway to CB-1 ceiling with continued coaching.
red_flags: Primary winning mechanism relies on physical dominance rather than refined processing, which may face challenges against elite NFL route runners. Development trajectory dependent on consistently improving route anticipation and processing speed. Limited sample against diverse offensive schemes.

capital_adjusted: R1 Day 2 (R2) - Tier B ±0.5 round range
strengths: Elite athleticism for the position with excellent movement skills in zone blocking schemes. Shows consistent power at the point of attack with ability to climb to second level and sustain blocks. High motor player with above-average awareness in pass protection.
red_flags: Limited experience against elite interior pass rushers due to Penn State's schedule. Production metrics don't fully match the athletic profile, suggesting some technical refinement needed. May struggle in pure gap schemes that require immediate anchor strength.

capital_adjusted: R2 - Tier B ±0.5 round range

red_flags: Limited counter package development creates FM-1 risk at NFL level where initial athleticism advantage diminishes. Run defense consistency wavers when asked to set edge against power schemes. Processing speed is reactive rather than anticipatory, leading to late recognition on RPO and misdirection concepts. Technique refinement needed to maximize physical tools.

capital_adjusted: R1 Day 2 (R2)

Demonstrates exceptional football IQ with ability to diagnose plays before snap and communicate adjustments to teammates. Strong production against high-level SEC competition with reliable tackling technique and gap discipline.
red_flags: Limited elite athleticism may restrict coverage range against faster receiving threats in NFL. Needs to improve lateral movement and change of direction to handle modern spread offenses. Some concerns about translating college production to NFL level without elite physical tools to compensate for processing advantages.

capital_adjusted: R1 Day 2 (R2) - Tier B ±0.5 round range
strengths: Elite route precision and separation creation at all three levels, with particularly strong timing on intermediate routes and comeback patterns. Demonstrates advanced processing of coverage concepts and consistently finds soft spots in zone coverage. Strong hands and ball security with minimal drops throughout college career.
red_flags: Limited elite speed creates ceiling concerns against top-tier NFL cornerbacks who can match his route running with superior athleticism. Lacks explosive after-catch ability and may struggle to create big plays without schematic advantages. Pre-combine measurables unknown, creating evaluation uncertainty around athletic baseline.

capital_adjusted: R1 Picks 25-32 / Top R2 - Tier B ±0.5 round range. Man coverage floor: confirmed. Conley Warning ceiling until competition performance resolves.
strengths: Elite physical tools with legitimate press coverage ability and size-speed combination that projects well to NFL boundary corner role. Shows consistent physicality at the catch point and has the athletic foundation to match up with bigger receivers at the next level.
red_flags: Reactive processing limits ceiling against elite route runners who can create separation through timing and technique rather than pure athleticism. Production decline against NFL-caliber competition raises questions about translating physical dominance to consistent coverage performance at the next level. Gareon Conley warning pattern active - athleticism mirage risk if processing doesn't develop.

capital_adjusted: R1 Picks 12-22 - FM-4 Medical Flag suppresses from mechanism-implied range. Tier B conditional - upgrades to Picks 11-20 if combine medicals confirm pre-injury movement quality intact
strengths: Elite anticipatory processing with confirmed CB-1 mechanism - reads routes before the break and positions himself perfectly in throwing lanes. Exceptional man coverage technique with press-man dominance at the line of scrimmage. Competitive toughness and assignment execution validated against NFL-caliber competition throughout Tennessee career.
red_flags: ACL tear in January 2025 resulted in missing the entire 2025 season, creating significant injury risk and movement quality concerns heading into the draft. Post-injury movement mechanics and explosion remain unconfirmed without combine/pro day testing. Character profile shows some gaps in psychological makeup and drive that could impact recovery timeline and developmental trajectory.

capital_adjusted: R2 — Tier B ±0.5 round range

red_flags: Athletic ceiling is limited compared to top-tier speed rushers, which may cap his pass rush upside against elite NFL tackles. Limited pre-combine measurables create uncertainty around true athletic baseline. Needs to prove he can win consistently when initial technique advantage is neutralized.

capital_adjusted: R2-R3 (Smith Rule -1 round), Tier B — ±0.5 round range

Processing and technique currently lag behind elite physical tools, creating inconsistent performance against top competition. capital_adjusted: R1 Day 2 (R2) - Tier B — ±0.5 round range
strengths: Bernard displays advanced route precision and timing, creating consistent separation through technique rather than pure speed. His processing ability allows him to find soft spots in zone coverage and adjust routes accordingly. Strong hands and catch reliability make him a trusted target in critical situations.
red_flags: Limited top-end speed may restrict vertical impact at the NFL level. Transfer from Washington creates some continuity questions in his development timeline. Production came primarily in Alabama's structured passing game, raising questions about creating separation against elite man coverage.

capital_adjusted: R2 - Tier B — ±0.5 round range
